sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
Now I see the pattern more clearly:
- Red at (26, 14): shifts row 26 LEFT by 1
- Green at (25, 48): shifts row 26 RIGHT by 1
- Red at (35, 14): shifts row 35 LEFT by 1
So the red shapes shift their corresponding row LEFT, and green shapes shift RIGHT. The middle pair affects row 26, the bottom pair affects row 35.
Now I need to figure out what the top red/green pair affects. The top red is at (17, 20) and top green is at (16, 39). These might affect the vertical columns.
But wait, I also need to understand what the goal is. The yellow markers are at (17, 32) and (38, 23). Let me think about what these markers mean...
Looking at the structure:
- Row 26 has 10 blocks at cols 17, 20, 23, 26, 29, 32, 35, 38, 41, 44
- Row 35 has 10 blocks at the same columns
- Col 23 has blocks at rows 17, 20, 23, 26, 29, 32, 35, 41, 44
- Col 35 has blocks at rows 17, 20, 23, 26, 29, 32, 35, 38, 41, 44
The yellow markers:
- (17, 32): This is at row 17, col 32. But col 32 is NOT one of the vertical columns (23 or 35). It's between them.
- (38, 23): This is at row 38, col 23. Row 38 is NOT one of the horizontal rows (26 or 35). It's between them.
Wait, let me reconsider. The yellow markers might indicate:
1. The target color for a specific position
2. The position where a block should be aligned
Actually, looking at the ascii again:
Row 17: cccccRRRcSScbbcppcYYcppcNNNcccccc
The YY is at some column. Let me count the characters...
Actually, I think the yellow markers are just 4px blocks at specific positions. They might indicate:
- (17, 32): The block at column 32 should match some target color
- (38, 23): The block at row 38 should match some target color
But wait, col 32 is part of row 26 (it's one of the 10 columns: 17, 20, 23, 26, 29, 32, 35, 38, 41, 44). And row 38 is part of col 35 (it's one of the rows: 17, 20, 23, 26, 29, 32, 35, 38, 41, 44).
So the yellow markers might indicate:
- (17, 32): The target for the block at column 32 in row 26 is yellow (Y)
- (38, 23): The target for the block at row 38 in column 23 is yellow (Y)
But that doesn't make sense because the blocks in the tracks don't include yellow colors...
Actually, wait. Let me reconsider the structure. The yellow markers might be:
- (17, 32): A marker indicating the target position for column 32
- (38, 23): A marker indicating the target position for row 38 in column 23
Hmm, let me think about this differently. Maybe the yellow markers indicate which positions need to have specific colors. The markers themselves are yellow, so maybe the goal is to make the blocks at those positions yellow?
But the blocks in the tracks don't include yellow... So maybe the goal is different.
Let me look at the yellow markers more carefully:
- (17, 32): This is at row 17, col 32. Row 17 is the top of the vertical columns. Col 32 is between cols 23 and 35.
- (38, 23): This is at row 38, col 23. Row 38 is between rows 35 and 41. Col 23 is the left vertical column.
Wait, I think I need to reconsider the structure. Let me look at the segmentation data again. The yellow blocks are at:
- (17, 32): A 4px yellow block
- (38, 23): A 4px yellow block
These might be target markers. The goal might be to shift the rows/columns so that specific colored blocks end up at the positions indicated by the yellow markers.
But (17, 32) is not on any track (row 26, row 35, col 23, or col 35). And (38, 23) is on col 23 but not on row 26 or 35.
Actually, wait. Let me reconsider. The yellow marker at (38, 23) IS on column 23. Column 23 has blocks at rows 17, 20, 23, 26, 29, 32, 35, 41, 44. Row 38 is NOT one of these rows. So (38, 23) is not on the track.
Hmm, but looking at the col 35 data, it has a block at row 38:
Col 35: p at row 17, b at row 20, S at row 23, g at row 26, w at row 29, g at row 32, p at row 35, w at row 38, b at row 41, g at row 44
So col 35 has 10 blocks, including one at row 38. But col 23 has 9 blocks, with a gap where row 38 would be.
Wait, let me re-check. The col 23 data was:
Col 23: S at row 17, p at row 20, g at row 23, b at row 26, w at row 29, w at row 32, b at row 35, g at row 41, S at row 44
That's 9 blocks. The gap is at row 38. So (38, 23) is where there's NO block currently, but there's a yellow marker.
Similarly, the yellow marker at (17, 32) is at row 17, col 32. Col 32 is not a vertical column (the vertical columns are 23 and 35). So (17, 32) is not on any track.
Wait, but row 26 has a block at col 32. So (17, 32) is at the same column as a block in row 26, but at a different row.
I think the yellow markers might indicate target positions. The goal might be to shift the rows/columns so that blocks end up at the yellow marker positions.
But that doesn't quite make sense either, because the yellow markers are at positions that are not on the tracks...
Let me try a different approach. Let me test the top red and green shapes to see what they do.

sections.content:
World model: Red shapes shift their row LEFT, green shapes shift RIGHT. Middle pair affects row 26, bottom pair affects row 35. Top pair likely affects vertical columns. Yellow markers at (17,32) and (38,23) are target positions.
Plan: Test top red shape at (17, 20) to see what it shifts.
